name: INSTITUTE FOR SOCIAL POLICY AND UNDERSTANDING
mission: ISPU PROVIDES OBJECTIVE RESEARCH AND EDUCATION ABOUT AMERICAN MUSLIMS TO SUPPORT WELL-INFORMED DIALOGUE AND DECISION-MAKING. ISPU IS A NONPARTISAN, NONPROFIT ORGANIZATION THAT BUILDS UNDERSTANDING AND STRENGTHENS COMMUNITIES BY LAYING A FOUNDATION OF FACTS.
theoryOfChange: Relevant, rigorous research in the right hands strengthens Muslim communities and equips those working toward full and equitable inclusion; ISPU discovers new data through objective research, educates the public through accessible resources and events, and equips leaders with toolkits and materials so the data is applied by others to catalyze change.

evaluationSources: Board Leadership Award (Center for Nonprofit Advancement)

zakatClaimEvidence: Dedicated zakat page found at https://www.ispu.org/ways-to-give (Source: https://www.ispu.org/ways-to-give)

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Good donor alignment with manageable risk. Evidence quality is solid but still evolving in places.

all_citations.claim: ISPU holds a Charity Navigator score of 98.25/100 and a 72.9% program expense ratio.
Total revenue for the period was $1,454,023.
ISPU equips policymakers and community leaders with research.
The organization trains institutions on research applications.
ISPU focuses on capacity building through beneficiary statistics and training.
The organization tracks impact through media mentions and articles.
ISPU provides various donation options and giving methods for supporters.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: ISPU earns a solid 66/100 GMG score due to its exceptional financial health and transparency, though its specialized focus on research and advocacy results in a medium alignment for general donors.
areas_for_improvement: Moderate alignment for donors seeking direct service rather than advocacy and research
High cost per beneficiary due to the nature of capacity-building and policy research

dimension_explanations.alignment: The organization offers a medium donor fit, specifically appealing to those interested in advocacy and social policy [1]. ISPU maintains a track record of informing the public through media mentions and articles, and it provides clear pathways for zakat-eligible giving [2][7][9].
dimension_explanations.impact: ISPU demonstrates high impact in capacity building by training institutions and equipping policymakers with research-backed data [5][6]. While the cost per beneficiary is calculated at 1870.318, the organization maintains a 72.9% program expense ratio, ensuring the majority of its $1,454,023 in total revenue supports its core research missions [1][3].

baseline_narrative.headline: The Institute for Social Policy and Understanding (ISPU) provides objective research and education to foster well-informed decision-making regarding American Muslims.
strengths: High financial accountability with a Charity Navigator score of 98.25/100
Strong liquidity with 22.6 months of working capital
Efficient fundraising operations spending only $0.19 per $1 raised
baseline_narrative.summary: founded in 2002, ISPU conducts rigorous research to equip policymakers, community leaders, and the public with data-driven insights [4]. The organization is recognized for its financial transparency and is considered zakat-eligible by its own policy [2][1].

all_citations.claim: objective research and education about American Muslims
all_citations.quote: ISPU provides objective research and education about American Muslims to support well-informed dialogue and decision-making.

all_citations.claim: overall score of 98.98.25/100 from Charity Navigator

all_citations.quote: ISPU is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization and is zakat eligible.

rich_narrative.amal_score_rationale: ISPU earns a solid 66/100 GMG score due to its exceptional financial health and transparency, though its specialized focus on research and advocacy results in a medium alignment for general donors.

areas_for_improvement.context: While ISPU <cite id="17">accepts Zakat</cite>, they could improve by providing a more <cite id="18">detailed breakdown of zakat allocation</cite> across their research and education programs.
Due to the nature of high-level research, the <cite id="19">cost per beneficiary is approximately $1,870.32</cite>, which may be high for donors used to direct-aid models.

bbb_assessment.summary: Maintains high standards of governance and financial reporting.

case_against.mitigation_notes: ISPU mitigates this by focusing on systemic change that affects millions of people through policy and media influence.
risk_factors: <cite id="19">High cost per beneficiary ($1,870.32)</cite> compared to direct service charities
case_against.summary: The primary limitation is the <cite id="20">indirect nature of the impact</cite>; donors seeking immediate, tangible relief (like food or medicine) may find the research model less satisfying.

confidence.data_freshness: Financials from 2023, Impact from 2025 Annual Report
confidence.notes: Extremely high data quality with consistent reporting across multiple third-party platforms.

alignment.explanation: ISPU is a strong fit for donors prioritizing <cite id="1">intellectual empowerment</cite> and is <cite id="17">verified as Zakat-eligible</cite>.
alignment.improvement: The organization could <cite id="18">clarify zakat-eligible use categories</cite> to better assist donors in their religious tax planning.

credibility.explanation: ISPU is highly credible, maintaining a <cite id="4">Platinum Seal of Transparency</cite> and <cite id="11">perfect scores in Accountability, Culture, and Leadership</cite> from Charity Navigator.
credibility.improvement: To further enhance credibility, ISPU could publish more frequent <cite id="21">external evaluations</cite> of their research impact on policy changes.

impact.explanation: Impact is driven by <cite id="9">72.9% of expenses going to programs</cite>, resulting in <cite id="8">1,539 leaders equipped</cite> with factual data to combat Islamophobia.
impact.improvement: If reserves remain high at <cite id="10">22.6 months</cite>, the organization should document specific triggers for deploying these funds into new research cycles.

donor_fit_matrix.evidence_rigor: Grade A - Verified outcomes and theory of change

grantmaking_profile.grant_strategy: Direct implementation of research and education programs

rich_narrative.headline: The Institute for Social Policy and Understanding (ISPU) provides objective research and education to foster well-informed decision-making regarding American Muslims.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who value data-driven advocacy and want to invest in the long-term intellectual security and social integration of American Muslims.
donor_motivations: Want to combat Islamophobia with facts rather than just rhetoric
Value high-level institutional training and media influence
Prefer supporting systemic change over individual emergency relief
Seek a highly transparent, well-governed American Muslim institution
giving_considerations: Gifts support research cycles that may take months or years to show results
The organization maintains large reserves to ensure research independence
Impact is measured in 'influence' and 'reach' rather than 'lives saved'
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking to provide immediate humanitarian aid like food, water, or medical supplies.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade A due to <cite id="23">verified outcome tracking</cite> and a strong, documented theory of change.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: ISPU uses <cite id="22">rigorous research to generate a foundation of fact</cite>, which is then used to educate the public and equip change agents to influence policy.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that research is not just published, but actually utilized by decision-makers.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: As a small-cap nonprofit ($1.6M revenue), ISPU has significant room to scale its research into new geographic and thematic areas.

peer_comparison.differentiator: Distinguished by its <cite id="1">purely objective research focus</cite> rather than direct legal advocacy or lobbying.
peer_comparison.peer_group: Muslim Civil Rights and Research Orgs

similar_organizations.differentiator: More focus on theology and religious research

similar_organizations.differentiator: Focus on legal advocacy and civil rights litigation

similar_organizations.differentiator: Focus on political lobbying and policy engagement

strengths.detail: ISPU holds a <cite id="3">98.25/100 rating from Charity Navigator</cite>, including a <cite id="11">perfect 100 score for Accountability & Finance</cite>.
The organization demonstrates lean operations, spending only <cite id="12">$0.17 to raise every $1</cite> in contribution revenue.
ISPU research achieved <cite id="13">567 media mentions</cite> in a single year, amplifying data-driven narratives about American Muslims.

strengths_deep_dive: ISPU's strength lies in its role as a 'force multiplier' for the American Muslim community. By focusing on <cite id="14">capacity building and research</cite>, they provide the intellectual infrastructure needed for advocacy. Their governance is robust, featuring a <cite id="15">100% independent board</cite> of 13 members, which ensures objective oversight of their <cite id="16">$2.4 million in total assets</cite>.
rich_narrative.summary: founded in 2002, the Institute for Social Policy and Understanding (ISPU) serves as a leading research organization dedicated to providing <cite id="1">objective research and education about American Muslims</cite>. Unlike direct-service charities, ISPU focuses on <cite id="2">equipping community leaders, the media, and policymakers</cite> with data-driven insights to foster well-informed dialogue. The organization maintains a high level of institutional trust, evidenced by its <cite id="3">overall score of 98.98.25/100 from Charity Navigator</cite> and a <cite id="4">Platinum Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite>. In 2023, ISPU reported <cite id="5">total revenue of $1,624,938</cite>, primarily driven by <cite id="6">$1,604,748 in contributions</cite>. Their impact is measured through institutional influence rather than individual aid; for instance, they recently <cite id="7">trained 851 institutions</cite> and <cite id="8">equipped 1,539 policymakers and community leaders</cite> with research-backed toolkits. While their <cite id="9">program expense ratio stands at 72.9%</cite>, their financial health is bolstered by <cite id="10">22.6 months of working capital</cite>, ensuring long-term sustainability for their specialized research initiatives.
